steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The claim is that people spend more time viewing art in a museum than online. To support this, a choice must show a concrete time difference between those two viewing contexts.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — three minutes at the museum vs. one minute online: Directly compares viewing time in both settings — museum time is longer, which is exactly what the claim needs. ✓
  • Choice B — whether other works are available online: Says nothing about how long people look at art. ✗
  • Choice C — where museum visitors come from: Visitor origin is irrelevant to viewing duration. ✗
  • Choice D — museum collection scope: How large a collection is tells us nothing about time spent viewing. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
